channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Reticulating node-linker-modulator chemical spaces for modular design of alkoxide-based glasses and liquids
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68591-1

Here the authors show a general method to make alkoxide-based network-forming glasses with tunable properties by combining titanium, zirconium and boron-based nodes, multi-dentate alcohol linkers and mono-dentate alcohol modulators.
item.dc:title: Reticulating node-linker-modulator chemical spaces for modular design of alkoxide-based glasses and liquids
item.dc:creator: Ying Liu
Yuqing Geng
Yanxin Deng
Chenghang Qi
Yidan Song
Shiyan Ren
Zezhao Xu
Rui-Fang Ma
Yihui Zhu
Zheng Yin
Ming-Hua Zeng
Shan Jiang
Gengxin Liu
Jianrong Zeng
Yingbo Zhao

item.title: Giant unusual anisotropic magnetoresistance enabled by hole-electron resonance in van der Waals heterostructures
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68438-9

Hole–electron resonance in WTe2 unlocks Coulomb-unconstrained spin transfer across WTe2/Fe3GaTe2, producing a giant 289% unusual anisotropic magnetoresistance and chiral transverse transport beyond conventional spin-transport models.
item.dc:title: Giant unusual anisotropic magnetoresistance enabled by hole-electron resonance in van der Waals heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Qian Chen
Yuxin Tian
Lei Wang
Chang Niu
Wei Jiang
Hainan Mao
Ya Zhai
Zhiyan Jia
Yong Jiang
Ke Xia

item.title: Real-time visualization of sub-particle reaction anisotropy via rotary-encoded scattering microscopy
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67721-5

This study reports an optical imaging approach that encodes structural information into scattering patterns, enabling real-time visualization of sub-particle reaction dynamics in liquid environments.
item.dc:title: Real-time visualization of sub-particle reaction anisotropy via rotary-encoded scattering microscopy
item.dc:creator: Jun-Hao Wan
Gang Wu
Chen Qian
Zheng-Yang Wang
Xian-Wei Liu

item.title: Spatiotemporally ordered topological transformation in layered double hydroxides enables synergistic mineralization of As<sup>III</sup>/Cd<sup>2+</sup>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68326-2

Here authors prepare a defect-rich Mg–Mn layered double hydroxide, which via a spatiotemporally ordered topotactic transformation, achieving synergistic comineralisation of AsIII and Cd2+.
item.dc:title: Spatiotemporally ordered topological transformation in layered double hydroxides enables synergistic mineralization of As<sup>III</sup>/Cd<sup>2+</sup>
item.dc:creator: Meiqi Zheng
Huanxu Du
Xiaoqing Cao
Si-Min Xu
Wei Chen
Wenying Shi
Xianggui Kong
Mingfei Shao
Xue Duan
